title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
summary: Here’s what happened in crypto today
briefing: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

title: Excessive AI spending risks global financial consequences, BIS warns
summary: Excessive AI spending risks global financial consequences, BIS warns
briefing: The AI investment surge is a potential flashpoint for systemic risk, “as financing has relied on enormous debt and highly leveraged nonbank structures that can rapidly unwind,” one analyst said in response to the report.

title: Dubai crypto market hits 50 licensed firms after new VARA approval
summary: Dubai crypto market hits 50 licensed firms after new VARA approval
briefing: Dubai’s VARA has licensed its 50th crypto firm, though only 39 VASPs were fully operational at the end of 2025 as the emirate’s regulated market continues to expand.

title: Pioneering zk-rollup Loopring closes DEX, citing lack of adoption
summary: Pioneering zk-rollup Loopring closes DEX, citing lack of adoption
briefing: “We lacked a virtual machine, no composability, no real‑world payment use cases. That limitation kept our ecosystem from growing,” said the team.

title: Ether treasury Sharplink bought $62.4M ETH last week
summary: Ether treasury Sharplink bought $62.4M ETH last week
briefing: Sharplink bought nearly 40,000 ETH last week after an eight-month pause, adding to signs the company has resumed its Ether accumulation strategy.

title: Bullish Bitcoin RSI divergence has analysts calling for 2022-style bear market bottom
summary: Bullish Bitcoin RSI divergence has analysts calling for 2022-style bear market bottom
briefing: Bitcoin RSI bullish divergences formed the basis for the new bull case, but some market takes warned that new BTC price lows were still to come.

title: Binance booted from EU, EthLabs rises up to save Ethereum: Hodler's Digest June 14-28
summary: Binance booted from EU, EthLabs rises up to save Ethereum: Hodler's Digest June 14-28
briefing: BitMine and Joe Lubin back a new non profit called Ethlabs that aims to spur adoption. Binance shutters services in Europe after failing to secure a license.

title: BIS warns stablecoins risk fragmenting global financial system
summary: BIS warns stablecoins risk fragmenting global financial system
briefing: The Basel-based institution said that private digital tokens fall short of the requirements for sound money and urged policymakers to accelerate work on tokenized forms of central bank and commercial bank money.

title: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
summary: The Future Cyberpunk Imagined Is Here: How Much Did It Get Right?
briefing: From AI to brain implants, cyberpunk got more right than many people realize. Its biggest prediction, however, wasn't the technology.

title: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
summary: The Stablecoin Founder Map Doesn't Match the Stablecoin Volume Map
briefing: Emerging markets drive most real-world stablecoin usage, yet founder concentration and venture funding remain U.S.- and Europe-centric.

title: Billionaire Jeremy Grantham Dismisses Bitcoin, Says Crypto Will Fade 'With a Whimper'
summary: Billionaire Jeremy Grantham Dismisses Bitcoin, Says Crypto Will Fade 'With a Whimper'
briefing: The seasoned investor has little faith in Bitcoin's staying power, expecting crypto to quietly fade away over time.

title: Wall Street's Next Tokenization Test: BlackRock-Backed Securitize's Market Debut
summary: Wall Street's Next Tokenization Test: BlackRock-Backed Securitize's Market Debut
briefing: Securitize expects to begin trading next week under the ticker symbol “SECZ,” following the completion of a merger with a blank-check firm.

title: Linux Foundation, Tech Giants Launch Akrites to Defend Open Source Against AI-Powered Attacks
summary: Linux Foundation, Tech Giants Launch Akrites to Defend Open Source Against AI-Powered Attacks
briefing: A coalition of 19 organizations—including every major AI lab and Wall Street banks—just built the security team that open-source maintainers never had.

title: Senators Demand Answers From CFTC Over Alleged Polymarket Deceptive Advertising
summary: Senators Demand Answers From CFTC Over Alleged Polymarket Deceptive Advertising
briefing: Senators are seeking answers from the CFTC about recent allegations of deceptive marketing from Polymarket.

title: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.6—But Only for Some Users Due to Trump Admin
summary: OpenAI Rolls Out GPT-5.6—But Only for Some Users Due to Trump Admin
briefing: OpenAI introduced the GPT-5.6 family of AI models on Friday, but only limited users can access them for now thanks to the U.S. government.

title: Spanish Regulator Says No Extensions for EU Crypto Deadline as Binance Remains Unlicensed
summary: Spanish Regulator Says No Extensions for EU Crypto Deadline as Binance Remains Unlicensed
briefing: Crypto firms operating in the European Union will be forced to cease operations if they do not obtain a MiCA license by July 1.
